text:
It was dusk. Myrna wanted to go bird-watching. She borrowed my camera.

We followed the creek. She told me birds were fascinating. I thought owls. She pointed.

That bird! I never saw one!

Myrna took fotos. We found hundreds. A snowy owl. A hawk.

Afterward we got treats. I knew her mom might get angry. I just promised I'd delete.

I looked silly. That turkey looked real me?

I did laugh. Why did I think pictures?

We took piccies. We wanted souvenirs.

I took mine home.

That thing came!

I screamed.

Myrna threw things. That's okay.

We lost it. But I got bird stuff. They liked my gift.

We talk every weekend.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
